[{"Name":"C G","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV-2U2BOwftTpE4-APOe7-eTMf0A6kaMa9FVvIu_LGJSAjoCcQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A beautiful book-cafe with fresh ingredients and great vibes!
We visited this charming, small book-cafe this morning and absolutely loved the atmosphere. The indoor space features a beautiful vintage decor filled with books, while the outdoor area has lovely small tables shaded by trees. The music was excellent and the service was very friendly.

Our coffee was quite good, and we tried the mushroom tart and the pastrami sandwich. The tart was delicious and the sandwich was highly prepared with visibly very fresh ingredients.

A small note/tip for the place: The menu stated that the sandwich contains "mustard sauce," but it actually had Dijon mustard. Since Dijon has a very distinct, sharp taste that is not for everyone (including us), it took away a bit from our enjoyment of the snack. It would be very helpful if this was explicitly mentioned on the menu. Overall, though, the quality was top-notch and we would definitely visit again!","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2Wd1Cj9qm5v1CZ_h4ybDcymfzOrVLGHT5X_pGrGLzeLe3dfA4eK-gCXiGt93bGpCVftq5fUZrRJKNOnV9NLzn6YBo7x-HQLKFoytd2cdxFrPjB3Ts0_bdSto5PHMiXT2Ao1CUb5x16q94=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0GQj-JY3I17C3C6arEql7CfHioYFQa2KgR8chxkub5ESO-JxSqfZYnPN--H3Dwle-S-ieLXnqyDY7H_g-ObzmlJ6wPVYhpyaVB3W5IlV2ubsE04jc4ATgN_0pdFKDlq5NfjePealpZa4eM=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1n3ad7W7YIt2fEbzLlHlTlFZ25x_TI-1iKrDLyH-6p_8_LGFyC3qgkdJl-tYeTvNn92UMA7i9eO1l4FlE_RJFqKBg2DWyICsMdZThOqjMvPGoTGDyCQiR7fDdUMSxKT0QuxrjLBaULARZN=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0WUoS_2v7Q39vZnkM3QuJFJSRAavNeQ-RkItNo7cW8UZ7QlFd2vFCyb1lHZuLpAAsmP6f4is7QKP-o1VCl6ihc9IgxSCrw7B8WHSN-zbfDcqk3uSQuMIi7JQeHE1mResqloZtQKvg_Pl4=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1q609SvwtMaPpcMnsfb9H6zHu-RIPQy51793AOf8yEpiUOeGXcJAGWXVFi3F9Dg3p46TKT9SmRR-svfews_0OMmUuVcKRwoL7py4EbgGgylh-Cf_7ApRQ8DsXuRGx8KZ0EIo8yUCe65Oer=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2H2g2SWIt_jblX9uL7oK9lDNX0POLqWMcgJZDwjLOQFNlF4kLM9j8g1LB4cUrvZxmvycl6CoMMDxJDUG4C2h_wHOX230-bb-14CEOjOWMrM_4Zl2m3mjegelNUjKlllgCGdp0Dm3UHHB7R=k-no"],"When":"a week ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2xsWlgya3pjMDh4V1dRMVIzVlViMkZDTVRWaU0xRRAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":4,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/103080446145545282418/reviews?hl=en","posted_at_unix_micros":1780741334351967,"updated_at_unix_micros":1780741549417649,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"A beautiful book-cafe with fresh ingredients and great vibes!

No big fuss - prefect coffee in a small funky place. You don't need more than that. Try watching them make the coffee - they treat it as art and it taste accordingly.","text_translated":"","published_at":"2025-12-08T19:36:21.149963Z"},{"Name":"Viale M","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK1fZt7b-WG0GIpQeuoB2NMU8F7uYMrYq0RGj0FOC2trwmywQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"**Have a cup, but leave the beans on the shelf.**

Taf Coffee seems to be suffering from an identity crisis. As a coffee shop, it is undistinguished from hundreds of others in Athens: pleasant, cozy, and enveloped in cigarette smoke.

As a roastery, it offers a staggering selection of coffee, predominantly from the Americas with a minority from Ethiopia. However, there is virtually nothing from Asia—no Indonesia (not even Java), no Vietnam, and none of the glorious coffee of India. This geographical gap could be excused if the American selection were consistently exceptional. Unfortunately, the sheer breadth of the selection results in a mixed experience. The flavor profiles are hard to decipher, with adjectives that seem drawn from the wild imagination of the roaster on any given day.

The staff, hurried by customers waiting for their espresso, was unhelpful and could offer little in the way of guidance or advice regarding the beans. To make matters worse, prices are exceptionally high, ranging from €50 to €150 per kilogram. At this price level, experimentation becomes very expensive, very quickly. Our best advice: come here for a cup of coffee, but leave the beans on the shelves.

As ordinary consumers, we are not paid or compensated in any way for this review. We aim to provide fair and reliable feedback for fellow coffee enthusiasts.","Images":null,"When":"5 months ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2tKWFRsVk5WelV3TmtaTWVFY3lPV1JSUm01VWNuYxAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":3,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/111417072343809195680/reviews?hl=en","posted_at_unix_micros":1767346343714444,"updated_at_unix_micros":1767346343714444,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"**Have a cup, but leave the beans on the shelf.**

[{"Name":"Jane L","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJstdsVnfDptflEAAdE-1HE1POEpn5VTKYW8nooDfebV4OrLQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We enjoyed our time at the café, which offers good coffee and a comfortably air-conditioned indoor seating area with pleasant décor. The outdoor seating is shaded by the balcony above, making it a comfortable spot even on a typical Greek summer day. So when me, a friend and my partner were looking to go somewhere to study and work, I suggested Kimolia since its also almost next to my own workplace and I've had good experiences in the past.
We proceed to sit down at the inside space with the couches, mind you the shop wasn't full and they didn't have a reservation in the couch space. We sat down to bemore comfortable, have some more space since their tables are tiny and uncomfortable and I have a back injury which causes me pain.
We sit down, the waiter comes asking if we expect more people and if not, we need to move IN CASE a big party comes in. After I was visibly displeased he continues to go and laugh about it with another server while also taking back the waters he brought.
After thinking for a while and seeing how uncomfortable the space he asked us to move to was, we decided to leave. Then another server comes by asking us if we're leaving and when we said yes she says its "understandable".
From the reviews I've seen, the management doesn't care a fair amount about the hospitality and the comfortness of their customers anymore and only wants tourists who will leave a big bill. 👉 Tate Speciality Coffee

📍 Kolokotrani 25, 10562 Athens, Greece

It is in the city center.

☕️ Nice latte macchiato.

Had the house blend, tried both espresso and long black. Also a side note that the prices displayed are for takeaway, and sit down prices are not displayed. According to the Takeaway menu displayed, you have to ask the staff for the Dine-in menu, rather odd, refer to photo of the menu board. You can really taste the quality in every sip, and it's the perfect spot for anyone who takes their coffee seriously. 🌟 Highly recommended if you're looking for a more "particular" brew!","text_translated":"","published_at":"2026-01-31T11:05:46.307053Z"},{"Name":"bruno korbar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWqZ1NjlvMkCQDlAbDFv59IsQY5tHKeO5Tj5HUZITeGZhM2eGLjGg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"“This place stresses me out” ~ my American co-traveler.

This is a magnificent symphony of chaos that really shouldn’t work, yet it produces excellent coffee and a really fun cultural experience.

First there is always a crowd, who all seemingly know the staff and yell something to them. They wave us in: “there is no line. You just order.”
So we did.
Then there is a wait. Baristas work in a mythical order, known only to them. Not inefficiently, as they are churning coffees left and right, but there always seems to be a wait involved, for the effect, I think.

Then there is a coffee. Their bean menu is extensive — some of the best I’ve seen in Greece (short of TAF and KROSS), but the preparation is somewhat erratic: no weighing, no measurement. Just the experience and love.
And this is, to some extent my biggest complaint:
Inconsistency. One of the ethiopan beans we had that they had dialed in was excellent, but one of their Colombian beans tasted over and under extracted in a way familiar to anyone who ever set the EK43 wrong.

There are many coffee shops that I gave 5 star review that were serving worse coffee. But this place has 5 star coffee, and they serve it in a 3 star way.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0N15FR8fl0uJkhLvmbOumMA5tdW78Nk939lOREIhWTgAoKNQF8HJ_lZx4ntiMzekbqmOWeMY-773NcYdg4kwXlw9Ci_wXKSny74TlNwyQQrajoCkwPtgPg0825alihWhfjgVqn1XBn5az_=k-no"],"When":"5 months ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2tJeVRHOTFUalZ0WjFVdE1tOHRhVGh5TW1SSldXYxAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":4,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/112549681460398473688/reviews?hl=en","posted_at_unix_micros":1766412951876671,"updated_at_unix_micros":1766412951876671,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"“This place stresses me out” ~ my American co-traveler.

Will definitely come here again next time I come.","text_translated":"","published_at":"2026-05-08T13:29:30.306058Z"},{"Name":"Tamara","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVVOY8DIFXhUGEUdccHKVhTwMsmSiFO0Im8S7VabcYFmi_thGU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Good specialty coffee, but kind of a chaotic experience and the coffee was a bit too dark for my taste.
They offer lots of bean options, so I asked for a light and fruity recommendation but my coffee still tasted like a darker roast. However, I really appreciate that they offer Sproud.
As for the chaotic part, the’s no proper line. People mill about the outside (since it’s kind of just a window and there’s no seating) and coffees seem to be served randomly (people that came after us received their coffee way before we did, but they were locals, so perhaps they serve locals first). There were only a few people waiting for coffee when we arrived, but since a number of other later arrivals were served first, we waited 20-30mins for a cappuccino and double espresso. We were pretty close to just walking away at that point because there was no acknowledgment or communication about the wait.
So overall not my favorite coffee experience in Athens.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2EtmCPNopSp9QBS6IztKnIxBFFhVVI0YKOAbsvuXulJmQUIUULwdFzWY8Bi77SqDuTwOT1b5AijXVgbfXjbJFal5RrwUDb0lnHx2DzGCCrmUfiHJbaIsKSqwh1EBJrIBbccjH3KI5QdfU=k-no"],"When":"5 months ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT21OVE5HOWtkRU42ZFZkS2NUZGZUWGhOYTBJeGVrRRAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":3,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/110055463846247996396/reviews?hl=en","posted_at_unix_micros":1766412386136993,"updated_at_unix_micros":1766412408097840,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"Good specialty coffee, but kind of a chaotic experience and the coffee was a bit too dark for my taste.

There were only a few people waiting for coffee when we arrived, but since a number of other later arrivals were served first, we waited 20-30mins for a cappuccino and double espresso. So overall not my favorite coffee experience in Athens.","text_translated":"","published_at":"2025-12-22T14:06:26.136993Z"},{"Name":"JohnTsikoudakis","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXuZ3Qc3NTkvDTlxSNrx4-401INTjhfStNAqf6vDbxLn9yN9h0M=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Great concept and nice speciality coffee options.

[{"Name":"Cindy NextOfCin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUZvZFGvggXDDApFruCMytZfP-Up9GN12sXWlkXu6abC8omCMU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We came here for lunch right after a museum tour and got there right when they opened, so we had no wait. This ended up being one of our favorite meals from our entire two weeks in Greece.

Everything we ordered was incredible. The tzatziki was hands down the best I’ve ever had. We also ordered the zucchini balls, meatballs in THE sauce, moussaka, and a warm drink that reminded me of mulled wine but somehow even better. Every single thing was a 10/10.  I think the waiter told us the meatball recipe has been in their family for 250 years?  I may have the years off but they definitely lived up to the hype. As good as my mom's.

I had moussaka three different times during our trip and this was by far the best one. Same with the meatballs — we tried them multiple times around Greece and these were easily our favorite. I was warmly welcomed by the man behind the bar, even though he didn’t speak English. Luckily, the other chess players who were there helped with translating and made me feel very welcome.

I could immediately sit down and play a game. I’m around 2300 Elo, and I found some fun opponents there. They even tried to find someone stronger for me to play against, but unfortunately nobody was available that day. I really appreciated the effort.

I also received some interesting background about the place, including the fact that Kasparov and Karpov have apparently played there in the past, which makes it feel like a very special spot for chess lovers.

The atmosphere is friendly and authentic, and the drinks are very cheap. I travel to a lot of places and I usually try to play chess in these places( Europe, Asia, Africa, South America). Today, in this "chess coffee", my wife and I were mistreated by the owner, who does not speak English. Since I was confused, cause I tried to pay for the bottle of water I consumed, I kindly asked the only 2 Greek "players" to explain what was happening. To my surprise they did not help (maybe it was a fantastic match, that couldn't stop the watch to help us). At the end, the owner knows the English word  "leave", and said that to me and my wife pushing us to the door (he did not accept my payment in cash, no cards). Very sad to experience this in Athens. Chess is more than a sport, some say it is a lifestyle, and this lifestyle overpasses race, color and language (if u follow chess history u know what I am saying). In my opinion and feeling, if you are not Greek do not go there. Only if you like to be mocked and sent to the street. I sincerely hope that these people one day really understand the meaning of chess, meanwhile let them move the pieces. We started with a cheese appetizer (not tzatziki), which was absolutely delicious. On top of that, we were pleasantly surprised to receive two complimentary spreads from the house to try, and they were both excellent.

We also ordered the meatballs, which were outstanding, but the highlight of the meal was definitely the slow-roasted lamb. It was so good that it hardly needs any further description other than simply saying it was fantastic.

The portions are very generous. Two starters and the lamb main course were almost too much for my wife and me to finish, even though we both usually eat well.

Prices are fair for the quality and quantity offered, so overall everything is highly commendable.

At the end of the meal, we were also treated to a traditional fruit liqueur on the house, which was a very nice and thoughtful gesture.

Finally, a special mention to the very friendly staff, who speak excellent English and made us feel very welcome. Authentic Greek food, and delicious favours. The meat was slow cooked to perfection, falling off the bones, with a side of fresh Greek salad and a lovely wine collection.

If you need a coffee break from Athens traffic, this is the perfect place!","text_translated":"","published_at":"2026-03-25T17:17:16.849294Z"},{"Name":"Kat K","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK3P-fTpip6OotiYH5DZhHcLNfIp7CU4EIJrxIAUJ08AfrNLA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"I visited today while travelling in Athens and was disappointed by the customer service.

After waiting several minutes for a menu, I was told that breakfast service had ended and was directed towards the toast options instead. This was only a few minutes after 3 pm, so I was surprised that the breakfast menu was no longer available.

As I wasn’t interested in toast, I left.

A short time later, after looking at the menu again on my phone, I returned and asked a staff member at the counter whether I could order a Caesar salad. She said yes and told me to take a seat.

When the original waitress later brought me a toasted sandwich, I explained that I had ordered a Caesar salad. She told me that I had ordered the toast, referring to my earlier visit. I politely explained that I had not ordered anything at that time and had left because I was told that only toast was available and I wasn’t interested in it.

Rather than simply clarifying the misunderstanding, I was then told that I should only have ordered through her. As a customer, I had no way of knowing this internal process.

The salad eventually arrived and the food itself was good. However, the interaction felt dismissive and unwelcoming throughout. When I paid for my meal, the same waitress did not acknowledge me, speak to me, or make eye contact, which reinforced the impression that I was not welcome.

As I was dining alone, the dismissive nature of the interaction left me feeling quite unsettled.

What makes this particularly disappointing is that the café itself is beautiful and the food was enjoyable. Sadly, the customer service overshadowed what could have been a great experience.","Images":null,"When":"2 weeks ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT21sVk5WRnZVV1JMUTBKd1RYbHRUMWt6V1RCR1VYYxAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":1,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/105929337343606850259/reviews?hl=en","posted_at_unix_micros":1780247881478460,"updated_at_unix_micros":1780247881478460,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"I visited today while travelling in Athens and was disappointed by the customer service.

[{"Name":"Mohammad Shabsogh","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW6GlFkMlGkqKIYa3iYw637O3w3kv5I_4d5Jmp34RM12ZeOjziI=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Clemente cafe is an exceptional experience cafe in Athens. As a frequent patron of cafes, I must say that my visit to Clemente cafe surpassed all of my expectations.

From the moment I walked in, I was greeted warmly by the attentive staff Sara. The cozy ambiance combined with the delightful aroma of freshly brewed coffee created the perfect setting for a relaxing afternoon. I was thoroughly impressed by the extensive menu options available, catering to a variety of tastes and dietary preferences.

I must highlight the impeccable service provided by Sara with her beautiful smile. Not only she knew about the menu offerings, but she also went above and beyond to ensure that my dining experience was nothing short of stellar. Every thing was truly impeccable, reflecting the care and attention to detail that went into crafting each item. Even the other staff were serving with a big smile.

Moreover, the quality of the food and beverages served at Clemente is truly top-notch. Every bite and sip were bursting with flavor. Whether you're in the mood for a perfectly brewed cappuccino or a decadent dessert, rest assured that Clemente will exceed your expectations.

In conclusion, I wholeheartedly recommend it to anyone in search of a memorable dining experience in Athens. The combination of exceptional service, inviting ambiance, and delectable offerings makes it a standout destination in the city's culinary scene.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3aXu2xL3G-8FKO4PQBPMPKJXXyxyed9qGQQYlagcUchb_v9fF8H2zGDL9dJM4Vi0FUgv5thoCJw5m8eP2O5iIwZaKzm3TWaKu7wv1GU-XFQKIFdHKIWMU2N7uBb72jSAXA7XimIA=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3wvOHtfTHHuc34E2PEF0iKVoquTspo4z8JNiO8bnVchtEmzEf2Tmy1_rd13yszuO6SzgwnsFXUPEx5CszjaoxZnLrhBmI3nDqtdGq0WOznlq4Zp-aj_o89IDiUfQgYQC4yEqAd=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3dbU_OpUiWqbEkWDq0HPUeQwGWbv_2Shgsy2cxg7-UPqiAmhpUSB2V2LIaXy9UyJEbwlM002b_tEviDtB8EKlKIbj5ljoT7I6u0__GOiOUjrYcRpCCR3IlokygHZ6bLzUx0nDBHA=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0dHFHooFY89BdvZWiyzHz9ufU9VDb_X-QLKT-A-saAechEODEvnunxrqD8Rmw1hGywJ5VyC7Kf5tZtrDYVct7-Oa0lh6GW4kAGLyDpYh7UCMGg1oB-BJpBvssp2jnwfLOzQ8h9=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0Q0NeXOoTgcUrUFstn8w9MgU3Z4EnHY205vxYcsZDU27z7K0qgwHe-JFUja6xLl_WH-u9kejlR5Q_YsvmXdYCeuwgXtDa2mZBeP-c8oklTlgi5TcATYxHXOv-T_kPO_onzoFcbZQ=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0oAxCNyWsyr284H2b_Issd0srVldoUQ3p7ux0jOQ-QG5ZJwU1IBIhRH0ssxGc6eqi7eVV8bQYq-MjrrztFzMfg0khBDDEif6s5GpXvlIyQAQfyV34aa89NmHu4XiEXZ_dNo7xn=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0z4jwrsX9cj2Ipjy78sMhPTWoQSzqNyUi6vMl4RqTr-iQjZmIQ-PixmpZ0Xxf6QxYYXIOKyBZ0IPq11B0uSOGTtOwcPV1O8drjn9qrtgFc6vcJyz1jFjdDpNelwAU6UlOt6ut_wQ=k-no"],"When":"2 years ago","review_id":"ChZDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnSUR6ajhLX1lBEAE","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":5,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/117640257172970034255/reviews?hl=en","posted_at_unix_micros":1718272570067895,"updated_at_unix_micros":1718272570067895,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"Clemente cafe is an exceptional experience cafe in Athens. When we visited, there was a strawberry galette and it was delicious. The sugar level was perfectly balanced, which is rare in Athens where many desserts can feel too sweet for those who prefer lighter flavors. This place gets that balance just right.

The coffee was good, though I personally prefer my coffee a bit hotter. Still enjoyable, just a personal taste. It is generally quite crowded, which says a lot about how popular it is.

The café sits inside an old passage, and that historic setting adds a beautiful charm to the atmosphere. The heart of Athens!

That's how I discovered one the most beautiful terrace in Athens !!
Weather wasn't my alley so I sat on the level below,on the 4th floor!
Surprise surprise!An old sewing workshop transformed in an all day bar restaurant!!!
Sat on the balcony, Acropolis standing in glory right across of me!

What a wonderful experience! The coffee and pastries were absolutely superb, but what impressed me most was the exceptional kindness and hospitality of the staff.

I ordered a fruit plate without thinking to ask what would be included. When it arrived, it contained peaches, which I’m allergic to. The staff couldn’t have been more gracious and understanding. The atmosphere is cozy and inviting, with a lovely patio where you can relax with a gentle fan blowing while watching the lively street scene. It’s the perfect place to enjoy a coffee, a pastry, or a leisurely breakfast.

When we sat down, they provided a bottle of water without mentioning that it'd be charged at €1.50, which we only discovered later.","text_translated":"","reply_text_original":"Hi Jane,
Thanks for taking the time to give us your 4* review. We’re glad to hear that our food was to your liking and that our service was friendly. We did just want to confirm that all of our pastries are baked fresh daily. Spanakopita (the Spinach Pie you mentioned) should be crispy on the outside and soft on the inside. Indeed if you ever find a Cheese or Spinach Pie with a soggy exterior, that would be a sign that it is less than fresh, something we would never serve. With the mention of the mineral water you were charged, we always serve bottled water (shown on the menu) as some visitors find the Athens tap water not to their liking, however, you are always welcome to ask for tap water when we come to your table and we’ll happily oblige – you will find most cafes of our quality and style in Athens serve bottled mineral water as standard.
We hope to be of service in the future. ","reply_language":"en","reply_posted_at_unix_micros":1753458803000000,"reply_updated_at_unix_micros":1753458803000000,"published_at":"2025-07-24T11:43:49.312487Z"}]

Kick in Kypseli is an absolute gem! The coffee is exceptional — rich, smooth, and clearly crafted with real skill and attention. Whether you’re in the mood for an espresso, cappuccino, or something more creative, everything tastes top-quality.

The baristas are incredibly friendly and welcoming, creating a relaxed neighborhood vibe that makes you want to come back again and again.

It’s also perfect for takeaway — quick service, consistent quality, and ideal when you’re on the go.

A standout spot in Kypseli for anyone who loves great coffee.

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Alex Serebrov","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU4lnJw-pXXZSSpA-iiZnSzONhQ-C0X3cGWpvI-zcDPVkNHZk4=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"​Klepsidra is a hidden gem that beautifully captures the true spirit of old Athens. It’s a charming little street cafe nestled right on a tiny staircase street in the historic area. I used it as a quick pit stop on my way to the Roman Agora, which is conveniently located right next to it.

The staff here are incredibly friendly and welcoming – they really go the extra mile to make you feel at home. The cocktails were delicious, perfectly mixed and full of flavor. The merchandise section is also impressive, with a large variety of pins, shirts, and unique souvenirs for collectors and fans alike.

I came here many times with my sweetheart and with people who truly mattered to me. I believe it was special for many others too — because of the marvelous view of the Acropolis from the first floor, and because of the coffee and the delicious Greek sweets.
Sadly, that feeling is gone. Today, the karydopita (walnut pie) was served on a cracked plate and the portion was disappointingly small (see photo). The Greek coffee was weaker than I remembered, and instead of tap water they served bottled water, as in restaurants. The Greek coffee was weaker than I remembered, and instead of tap water they served bottled water, as in restaurants. For a traditional Greek café, a kafeneion, this is really unusual and clearly about charging more.

Finding Couleur Locale in the winding alleys of Monastiraki feels like uncovering a well-kept secret in the heart of Athens. Tucked away on Normanou Street, this vibrant rooftop bar offers an unmatched, panoramic view of the Acropolis and the Parthenon that truly comes alive as the sun sets.
Atmosphere & Vibe
The venue spans several floors, with the rooftop terrace on the 3rd floor serving as the ultimate highlight.
Energy: It masterfully blends a bohemian, retro-chic interior with a modern, lively energy.
Music: The background is often set by a live DJ spinning a mix of house, jazz, soul, and rock'n'roll, creating a chill yet trendy environment.
Local Feel: Unlike many typical tourist spots, it maintains a strong "local color," attracting a fashionable crowd of young Athenians.
Food & Drink Highlights
The menu at Couleur Locale focuses on high-quality street food and expertly crafted beverages.
Craft Cocktails: Known for their creative and unique flavors, the bar's signature cocktails are a must-try.
Savory Bites: Popular dishes include the juicy burgers, chicken skewers with sesame, and the halloumi, grilled vegetable, and fig balsamic sandwich.
Brunch & Snacks: Visitors also rave about the generous pancakes and traditional loukoumades (Greek doughnuts) served with Good food, good drinks, okay pricing 👍🏼","text_translated":"","published_at":"2026-05-27T16:55:22.845159Z"},{"Name":"Lesia Zofia Dluzniewska","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX9blayDwJ8F9pEcQnBkFIap8cSpVFicLsSLZVzqgMIFylMvsml=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"I’ve been to Couleur Locale a few times 3-4 years ago and was always happy about the food and service, however today’s visit was beyond disappointing.

I’ll start with the positives:
When we arrived at 10:50 for brunch there was only one big group of around 10 people and us (2 people). At the time there was only one waitress but we were immediately served with water and she took our orders. We received our coffees and juice very fast, all 3 very tasty. The waitress was also very nice and the view on Acropolis was too.

That’s where the positives end. We ordered our breakfast (toast with fried eggs - first position on the menu) and waited 50 MINUTES for the food to arrive. The place wasn’t that full and noone can tell me that preparing this easy brunch takes that long. Each ingredient of the food tasted good, however the meal altogether was too heavy, too salty and too oily and we weren’t able to finish it as we already felt sick. I think adding more healthy stuff to the whole meal would balance it more.

After that we had to wait another 20mins for waitress to notice us wanting to pay.

Unfortunately we spent more than 1.5h in that place which to me is too much if most of the time is waiting

We definitely won’t be back again","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3TRD7lPhm7pFMgSaK3pDdUAZFQ86rMvZ3_hRWiduvcftjpbQmkImzyWL2qTub8S4qNj4k0rwt0Yfn7GeL58jwzR9qd6zPoeq90uKnC4BhZKbUwdql14sXuxjvccIqM7W4OOy3xYCR9Vjy9=k-no"],"When":"Edited 8 months ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT2tZMFYyZFlOMDlSV2w5R1oyTmxZemRxYjNwcFRIYxAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":2,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/101920520813786284972/reviews?hl=en","posted_at_unix_micros":1760435322292412,"updated_at_unix_micros":1760531431900845,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"I’ve been to Couleur Locale a few times 3-4 years ago and was always happy about the food and service, however today’s visit was beyond disappointing.

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Mia Gilcrest","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW9Ty7aT_AgNQnyTglhxXbQ-AGU1fNC5AA_ErO2QmJ4ZM8EVHA=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Running to reviews because I’m familiar with the receipt on the table rule here in Greece. I’ve done my fair share of time out of the states. What I haven’t attempted yet is to grab coffee with a friend from Wild Souls and buy one THEIR treats while having a yogurt cup packed with me so my sugar levels don’t drop just in case their treats weren’t good. I was wrong because it’s their coffee that’s not good. Bland no flavor even when you ask medium sweet it tastes like milk with a coffee flavor of 1 bean. After we got our items we sat down. A worker walks out and says we cannot sit there because of my yogurt. No further explanations from her. Confused why, I politely say even if we purchased cafe items and I held up the treat and pointed at our coffee. Her voice changed immediately like somehow asking educational cafe questions is punished with attitude. I wanted to know what i was doing wrong to avoid error next time. She said NO I have a problem, very stubbornly. I submitted to her because she genuinely scared me. Don’t worry I researched what I did wrong on Ai just like their Ai instagram. So if you have health issues, need some sugar, and a seat before you drop. Stay far away from this manager. She will not understand and assume the worst of you. Probably because I’m a (Greek) American. Had receipts for all my items even my yogurt cup.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3AwIPsH2o5CRtZunOsq5KQksiOhwVN2RPZsrvjZGBYMjM7jsjK_hM6r-aH5Vvj2uPHwYyGwZCcG0-l08gJ2HDppzT90KczzVpy8oWfPMzNTg9tbKCP_ZSOnclQ479VEybpS0Z2JEt6CVIj=k-no"],"When":"a month ago","review_id":"Ci9DQUlRQUNvZENodHljRjlvT25SaU9EaDBUbWx1WTBvMWNFSkZORzE2VG5GNlEzYxAB","source":"Google","rating_scale":5,"rating_float":1,"author_url":"https://www.google.com/maps/contrib/101694371938000102068/reviews?hl=en","posted_at_unix_micros":1777888222578912,"updated_at_unix_micros":1777888222578912,"language":"en","translated_lang":"","text_original":"Running to reviews because I’m familiar with the receipt on the table rule here in Greece. The breathtaking beauty and magical decorations completely immerse you in a legendary atmosphere, making you feel like you've stepped right into an animated movie. Even the waiters are dressed in costumes that perfectly match the theme

To get the full experience, you really need to sit down for a drink or a bite. I initially went just to take some photos and leave, but I couldn't resist diving into the whole experience. I ordered breakfast—even though I wasn't even hungry—and it turned out to be absolutely delicious and reasonably priced! The staff were also incredibly sweet and welcoming

The view is absolutely stunning and easily 5/5. The staff were also genuinely lovely and attentive.

Foodwise, it was a mixed experience. The octopus carpaccio was okay at best with a lingering fishy taste that was not very pleasant. The soup of the day sweet potato was the highlight and truly felt like a warm hug in a bowl.

We ordered the fish of the day and while the Chateaux Lazaridi (white) wine paired well with it, for €95 we were disappointed to be served only a single filleted side instead of the whole fish. The fish was grilled well but nothing memorable, and the marinara pasta that came with it was decent but nothing special.

Overall, it was a nice experience for the ambiance and scenery, but the food did not live up to expectations for the price. I was very lucky to have a place so close. Happy blender was a hop, skip and jump away.  I chose the ACAI energy bowl with the addition of Blueberries and peanut butter sauce for more protein.  The Mrs bought the ACAI Island bowl. We both loved our bowls.  We had a good chat to the owners of the shop to talk about the difference of the acai bowls from here and back home in Australia.  Were so used to having more frozen acai compared to the fresh made from here.  I loved it.  I normally have 1 or 2 soft serve acai a week back home.  I will most likely visit again tonight before they close to have a last one in Greece.
